title: Will President Trump send ICE agents to Boston’s Logan Airport?
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: President Trump plans to deploy ICE agents to airports, including possibly Boston's Logan, amid TSA staffing shortages caused by a government shutdown.
AFGE Local 2617 president Mike Gayzagian states it's unclear if ICE will be sent to Logan Airport as New England has fewer issues.
Logan Airport, so far unaffected by the shutdown, reports normal operations and has no confirmation of incoming ICE agent deployment.

title: Is Bruins goalie Jeremy Swayman a legitimate Vezina candidate? The numbers speak for themselves.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman is a key factor in the Bruins' playoff push, excelling in critical games like against the Red Wings.
In 47 games this season, Swayman ranks second in the NHL for goals saved above expected with 27.1.
The Bruins face defensive challenges, allowing high shot and scoring chances, but Swayman consistently mitigates these threats.

title: Celtics legend Paul Pierce continues to make a case for Jaylen Brown as NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce advocates for Jaylen Brown as NBA MVP, citing his stellar performance for the Celtics this season.
Jaylen Brown's career-high averages: 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game, as noted by Pierce.
Celtics' season success with Tatum returning could impact MVP recognition, according to Paul Pierce's remarks.

title: Plymouth police officer on paid leave amid criminal investigation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Plymouth police officer Samantha Pelrine is on paid leave amid a criminal investigation by Massachusetts State Police.
The investigation into Pelrine's off-duty conduct prompted both State Police and an internal police department inquiry.
Plymouth police stress accountability, noting violations of department policy or state law will not be tolerated.

title: MLB writer predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony predicted to win AL MVP in 2026 by The Athletic's Eno Sarris.
Anthony's rookie season stats: .292 average, eight HRs, 32 RBI, .859 OPS over 71 games.
Impressed during 2026 World Baseball Classic, batting .280 with two HRs and seven RBI.

title: Pedestrian killed in Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was killed in a hit-and-run incident near Southampton and Bradston streets in Roxbury, Friday morning.
The victim was taken to a local hospital where they were pronounced dead, according to Boston police.
The driver fled the scene, but the vehicle was found; no arrests have been made and the investigation is ongoing.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about chances for snow through Monday
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Snow showers could lead to a coating to 2 inches of wet snow near I-90 through Monday night.
Rain transitioning to wet snow is expected from northern Massachusetts to Connecticut, Rhode Island, and parts of Southeast Massachusetts.
Meteorologists predict accumulations mainly of coating to 1 inch, with up to 2 inches possible in steady snow bands.

title: Mass. police officer arrested for OUI, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Norwood Police Officer Shawn Willman was arrested for OUI while off duty, the department announced on Sunday.
Willman has been placed on administrative leave pending the outcome of the criminal case and an internal investigation.
No court information for Shawn Willman was available as of Sunday evening, police said.

title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, allegedly stabbed his friend, Brian Gordon, during a Miami Beach bachelor party altercation.
Both Dickerson and Gordon were injured and taken to the hospital; Dickerson remains held without bond.
The altercation reportedly began over women at bars, with Gordon claiming Dickerson threatened him with a knife.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, marking their third defeat in four games this season.
An early goal by Ilay Feingold gave New England a 1-0 lead, but turnovers led to St. Louis's comeback.
New England's forward Leo Campana exited due to an injury; its nature and severity remain undetermined.
